_doc: Managed settings for all Bubble VPS agents ({{OPERATOR}} msg 3611, 2026-06-01). Deployed root-owned to /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json. Per the Anthropic docs this layer CANNOT be overridden by user/project settings OR command-line args (incl. --dangerously-skip-permissions) — verified empirically: a deny rule here blocked a Read even under --dangerously-skip-permissions. Purpose: (1) the mission-file-guard PreToolUse hook lives here so an agent (or a prompt-injection) CANNOT disable it by editing user/project settings; (2) deny rules hard-block read/edit of secrets + cross-agent runtime env + the guard/settings that protect everything. NOT using allowManagedHooksOnly because each dept has its own per-path SessionStart hook that must keep loading — putting the guard in managed (un-removable) achieves the goal without breaking those. This is the Claude-Code-level layer; OS-level enforcement vs raw subprocesses comes from the sandbox (separate, follow-up piece). See ~/.claude/agent-memory/rnd/reference_vps_agent_hardening_layers.md.

PostToolUse._doc: Card #732 — root fix for #695 (key-name self-redact missed a secret) and #726 (211 transcripts leaking token values in cleartext). Matcher '.*' = ALL tools, since the leak surface is any tool_result, not just Edit/Write/Bash. Scrubs secret-VALUE-shaped strings (sk-ant-*, sk-or-v1-*, tskey-auth-*, etc — NOT key-name matching) out of tool_output before it reaches the model. UNVERIFIED whether updatedToolOutput also persists to the on-disk .jsonl transcript — see output-guard.py header. `timeout` is in SECONDS per the hooks doc, not ms — 10 means fail-open kicks in after 10s, not ~2.8h. NOTE: the PreToolUse mission-guard block above previously had `timeout: 10000` (same units bug, ~2.8h instead of 10s) — fixed to `10` in card #734. Root-owned at /opt so a dept agent (or a prompt-injection) can't disable it — same placement pattern as the mission-guard above.
